caption_gen:
The image shows a biopsy of skin. The most important histological features include atypical melanocytes infiltrating the dermis.
Final diagnosis: MELANOMA

Melanoma.
The image shows irregular nests of melanocytes with atypical nuclei and prominent nucleoli, along with scattered atypical melanocytes infiltrating the dermis.
MELANOMA
Melanoma. Basal cell hyperplasia and irregular pigmentation are noted. Final diagnosis: MELANOMA.

The image shows a skin biopsy with a predominantly melanocytic proliferation. The most important histological features include atypical melanocytes with irregular nuclear contours and prominent nucleoli, along with an asymmetric growth pattern and irregular pigment distribution.
Final diagnosis: MELANOMA

Melanocytic proliferation with atypical features.
Final diagnosis: MELANOMA

The image shows a dense proliferation of spindle cells.
Final diagnosis: MELANOMA

Melanoma features include atypical melanocytes and irregular pigment patterns.
Final diagnosis: MELANOMA

The image shows a section of skin with a dense collection of melanocytes. These cells are arranged in nests and sheets, with some variation in size and shape.
Final diagnosis: MELANOMA

The image shows a biopsy with features including atypical melanocytes and irregular nests.
Final diagnosis: MELANOMA

The image shows a skin biopsy with multiple atypical melanocytic nests.
Final diagnosis: MELANOMA
